heading: Powers, duties, and responsibilities of the First Responder Network Authority
label: 1426 Powers, duties, and responsibilities of the First Responder Network Authority

children.text: To exercise, through the actions of its Board, all powers specifically granted by the provisions of this subchapter, and such incidental powers as shall be necessary.

To hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the First Responder Network Authority considers necessary to carry out its responsibilities and duties.
To obtain grants and funds from and make contracts with individuals, private companies, organizations, institutions, and Federal, State, regional, and local agencies.

To accept, hold, administer, and utilize gifts, donations, and bequests of property, both real and personal, for the purposes of aiding or facilitating the work of the First Responder Network Authority.
To spend funds under paragraph (3) in a manner authorized by the Board, but only for purposes that will advance or enhance public safety communications consistent with this chapter.

To take such other actions as the First Responder Network Authority (through the Board) may from time to time determine necessary, appropriate, or advisable to accomplish the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Duty and responsibility to deploy and operate a nationwide public safety broadband network
children.label: b Duty and responsibility to deploy and operate a nationwide public safety broadband network
A
children.text: ensuring nationwide standards for use and access of the network;

children.label: B
children.text: issuing open, transparent, and competitive requests for proposals to private sector entities for the purposes of building, operating, and maintaining the network that use, without materially changing, the minimum technical requirements developed under ;section 1423 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: encouraging that such requests leverage, to the maximum extent economically desirable, existing commercial wireless infrastructure to speed deployment of the network; and

children.label: D
children.text: managing and overseeing the implementation and execution of contracts or agreements with non-Federal entities to build, operate, and maintain the network.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure the safety, security, and resiliency of the network, including requirements for protecting and monitoring the network to protect against cyberattack;

children.label: i
children.text: built to open, non-proprietary, commercially available standards;

children.label: ii
children.text: capable of being used by any public safety entity and by multiple vendors across all public safety broadband networks operating in the 700 MHz band; and

children.label: iii
children.text: backward-compatible with existing commercial networks to the extent that such capabilities are necessary and technically and economically reasonable;

children.label: C
children.text: promote integration of the network with public safety answering points or their equivalent; and

children.label: D
children.text: address special considerations for areas or regions with unique homeland security or national security needs.

children.heading: Rural coverage
children.label: 3 Rural coverage
children.text: In carrying out the duties and responsibilities of this subsection, including issuing requests for proposals, the nationwide, interoperable public safety broadband network, consistent with the license granted under , shall require deployment phases with substantial rural coverage milestones as part of each phase of the construction and deployment of the network. To the maximum extent economically desirable, such proposals shall include partnerships with existing commercial mobile providers to utilize cost-effective opportunities to speed deployment in rural areas.section 1421 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: obtain grants from and make contracts with individuals, private companies, and Federal, State, regional, and local agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: hire or accept voluntary services of consultants, experts, advisory boards, and panels to aid the First Responder Network Authority in carrying out such duties and responsibilities;

children.label: i
children.text: network capacity licensed to the First Responder Network Authority; and

children.label: ii
children.text: network infrastructure constructed, owned, or operated by the First Responder Network Authority; and

children.label: D
children.text: take such other actions as may be necessary to accomplish the purposes set forth in this subsection.

children.label: C
children.text: practices, procedures, and standards for the management and operation of such network;

children.label: D
children.text: terms of service for the use of such network, including billing practices; and

children.label: i
children.text: management and operation of such network;

children.label: ii
children.text: practices and procedures of the entities operating on and the personnel using such network; and

children.label: iii
children.text: necessary training needs of network operators and users.

children.label: i
children.text: construction of a core network and any radio access network build out;

children.label: ii

children.label: vi

children.heading: Method of consultation
children.label: B Method of consultation
children.text: The consultation required under subparagraph (A) shall occur between the First Responder Network Authority and the single officer or governmental body designated under .section 1442(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: commercial or other communications infrastructure; and

children.label: B
children.text: Federal, State, tribal, or local infrastructure.

children.heading: Maintenance and upgrades
children.label: 4 Maintenance and upgrades
children.text: The First Responder Network Authority shall ensure the maintenance, operation, and improvement of the nationwide public safety broadband network, including by ensuring that the First Responder Network Authority updates and revises any policies established under paragraph (1) to take into account new and evolving technologies.

children.heading: Roaming agreements
children.label: 5 Roaming agreements
children.text: The First Responder Network Authority shall negotiate and enter into, as it determines appropriate, roaming agreements with commercial network providers to allow the nationwide public safety broadband network to roam onto commercial networks and gain prioritization of public safety communications over such networks in times of an emergency.

children.heading: Network infrastructure and device criteria
children.label: 6 Network infrastructure and device criteria
children.text: The Director of NIST, in consultation with the First Responder Network Authority and the Commission, shall ensure the development of a list of certified devices and components meeting appropriate protocols and standards for public safety entities and commercial vendors to adhere to, if such entities or vendors seek to have access to, use of, or compatibility with the nationwide public safety broadband network.

children.heading: Representation before standard setting entities
children.label: 7 Representation before standard setting entities
children.text: The First Responder Network Authority, in consultation with the Director of NIST, the Commission, and the public safety advisory committee established under , shall represent the interests of public safety users of the nationwide public safety broadband network before any proceeding, negotiation, or other matter in which a standards organization, standards body, standards development organization, or any other recognized standards-setting entity addresses the development of standards relating to interoperability.section 1425(a) of this title

children.heading: Prohibition on negotiation with foreign governments
children.label: 8 Prohibition on negotiation with foreign governments
children.text: The First Responder Network Authority shall not have the authority to negotiate or enter into any agreements with a foreign government on behalf of the United States.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund to be known as the “Network Construction Fund”.

children.label: A
children.text: First Responder Network Authority to carry out this section, except for administrative expenses; and

children.label: B
children.text: NTIA to make grants to States under .section 1442(e)(3)(C)(iii)(I) of this title

children.heading: Termination of Authority
children.label: f Termination of Authority
children.text: The authority of the First Responder Network Authority shall terminate on the date that is 15 years after .February 22, 2012

children.heading: GAO report
children.label: g GAO report
children.text: Not later than 10 years after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to Congress a report on what action Congress should take regarding the 15-year sunset of authority under subsection (f).February 22, 2012
